basics.name: Michael Eniolade
basics.label: Software Engineer / Data Scientist
basics.summary: Software Engineer/Data Scientist with 10+ years of experience designing, building, and deploying data pipelines, machine learning models, and cloud-native solutions across healthcare, telecom, government, and education sectors.

work.name: Mercor Intelligence
highlights: Designed ambiguity-focused evaluation specifications for AI coding models.
Annotated SWE-Bench Pro V2 fail-to-pass tests by analysing requirements, implementation code, and test expectations.
Evaluated AI-generated patches without code execution by comparing failure diffs against test expectations.
Conducted structured A/B evaluations of AI coding agents on production-realistic TypeScript monorepo tasks.

work.name: Handshake
highlights: Collaborate with AI researchers to evaluate and refine reasoning systems.
Develop and modify Python-based machine learning workflows using scikit-learn and PyTorch.
Engineered multi-trap benchmarks for senior backend engineering evaluation.

work.name: Professional Development
highlights: Advanced technical expertise in AWS, Azure, GCP, dbt, PySpark, Airflow, Docker, Kubernetes, and MLflow.
Pursuing Ph.D. in Information Technology at University of the Cumberlands.

work.name: Pyramid Systems Inc.
highlights: Automated AI/ML pipelines using PySpark, Airflow, Delta Lake, and dbt, improving development efficiency by 30%.
Built and demoed an AI-powered chatbot POC integrating Python APIs, Hugging Face Transformers, LangChain, and MLflow.
Enhanced data infrastructure by implementing CI/CD workflows (GitHub Actions, Docker, Kubernetes).

work.name: UnitedHealth Group (UHG)
highlights: Improved claims platform reliability by resolving SLA-critical incidents with AWS CloudWatch and Jenkins, cutting downtime by 25%.
Performed SQL-based data validations, backend tuning, and automated batch workflows.

work.name: Deloitte LLP
highlights: Executed ETL pipelines and SQL-based validations for enterprise migration projects.
Developed backend test plans and automation checks, reducing post-migration defects by 20%.

work.name: Dept. of Human Services, State of Oregon
highlights: Built automation scripts for system shakeouts, cutting manual time by 40%.
Improved system reliability through root cause analysis and nightly monitoring.

work.name: Edmin Inc.
highlights: Automated Python-based analysis workflows, reducing manual effort by 25%.
Delivered Tableau dashboards that accelerated decision-making.

work.name: Speedway Yearbooks
highlights: Conducted system testing and KPI monitoring, improving deployment success rates.
Implemented real-time dashboards for performance tracking.

work.name: Rhythex Consulting
highlights: Automated monitoring scripts for billing anomaly detection.
Improved data accuracy and reduced downtime across telecom pipelines.

work.name: Etisalat
highlights: Supported billing system reliability with automated alerts and B2B data validation.
Reduced downtime by optimizing recurring processes.

volunteer.summary: Coached early-career professionals on self-management, problem-solving, and adaptability.
Guided mentees in IT troubleshooting and proactive system support, fostering career readiness.

highlights: Focus on applying AI and machine learning to healthcare cybersecurity, anomaly detection, and IoT security.
